---
title: "5d: Gene Alignment Visualization"
format:
  pdf:
    toc: true
execute:
  echo: false
---

## Summary

Gene order standardization aligns genes across Complete genomes using anchor genes
(genes appearing exactly once in every strain). Strains are grouped by gene order
consistency, reversed if needed, and rotated to start at gene 1.

```{python}
import pandas as pd
summary = pd.read_csv("../output/data/5d_gene_alignment_summary.csv")
for _, row in summary.iterrows():
    print(f"  {row['metric']}: {int(row['value'])}")
```

## Genetic Variation

Variation types identified by comparing each strain's gene order to the reference.

```{python}
variation = pd.read_csv("../output/data/5d_genetic_variation.csv")
print(f"Total strains analyzed: {len(variation)}\n")
print(variation["Variation"].value_counts().to_string())
```

## Gene Length Distribution

Histogram of gene counts per Complete genome after GFF parsing.

![](../output/figures/5d_gene_length_distribution.png){width=80%}

## Circular Genome Plots

Circular plots show gene positions for each phylon. Orange/coral segments indicate
phylon-specific genes; blue segments indicate anchor genes (conserved single-copy
genes used for ordering).

```{python}
#| output: asis
from pathlib import Path
circ_files = sorted(Path("../output/figures/").glob("5d_circular_*.png"))
for cf in circ_files:
    phylon = cf.stem.replace("5d_circular_", "")
    print(f"### {phylon}\n")
    print(f"![](../{cf}){{width=70%}}\n")
```

## Unique Genes by Phylon

Genes that belong exclusively to a single phylon (not shared with any other).

```{python}
unique = pd.read_csv("../output/data/5d_unique_genes_by_phylon.csv")
print(f"Total unique gene-phylon pairs: {len(unique)}\n")
print("Top 10 phylons by unique gene count:")
print(unique.groupby("phylon").size().sort_values(ascending=False).head(10).to_string())
```
